children.name: Colostethus
children.description: Colostethus — a genus of poison dart frogs in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Colostethus agilis
children.description: Colostethus agilis is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae. It is endemic to Colombia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist montane forests and rivers.

children.name: Colostethus alacris
children.description: Leucostethus alacris or the Finca Primavera rocket frog is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae. It is endemic to Colombia.

children.name: Colostethus dysprosium
children.description: Leucostethus dysprosium is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae. It is endemic to Antioquia, Colombia.

children.name: Colostethus furviventris
children.description: Colostethus furviventris is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae. It is endemic to Colombia. This frog is found on the leaf litter in cloud forests.

children.name: Colostethus imbricolus
children.description: Colostethus imbricolus is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae. It is endemic to Colombia.

children.name: Colostethus inguinalis
children.description: Colostethus inguinalis is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae. It is endemic to northwestern and northcentral Colombia. Its vernacular name is common rocket frog, although this name can also refer to Colostethus panamansis that until 2004 was considered a junior synonym of Colostethus inguinalis.

children.name: Colostethus jacobuspetersi
children.description: Colostethus jacobuspetersi, commonly known as the Quito rocket frog, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Colostethus latinasus
children.description: Colostethus latinasus is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae. It is known from Cerro Pirre in Darién Province, Panama, from Chocó Department in adjacent Colombia, and from Tierralta, Córdoba Department, Colombia.

children.name: Colostethus lynchi
children.description: Colostethus lynchi is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae. It is endemic to Colombia.

children.name: Colostethus mertensi
children.description: Colostethus mertensi is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae, Endemic to Colombia.

children.name: Colostethus panamansis
children.description: Colostethus panamansis, also known as the Panama rocket frog or (ambiguously) common rocket frog, is a species of poison dart frog.

children.name: Colostethus pratti
children.description: Colostethus pratti is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Colostethus spec
children.description: Colostethus spec — a species in the genus Colostethus, family Dendrobatidae. Also known as poison dart frogs.

children.name: Colostethus thorntoni
children.description: Colostethus thorntoni is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Colostethus ucumari
children.description: Colostethus ucumari is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Colostethus sp.
children.description: This species is a new addition to the diverse fauna of South American forests, known for its striking color patterns and cryptic behavior.

children.name: Excidobates
children.description: Excidobates — a genus of poison dart frogs in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Excidobates captivus
children.description: Excidobates captivus, the Santiago poison frog It is endemic to northwestern Peru and southern Ecuador.

children.name: Excidobates condor
children.description: Excidobates condor, the Cóndor poison frog, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Excidobates sp.
children.description: This species is a rare find in the montane forests of South America, known for its unique coloration and elusive behavior.

children.name: Ameerega
children.description: Ameerega — a genus of poison dart frogs in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Ameerega altamazonica
children.description: Ameerega altamazonica is a small species of poison dart frog that lives in central Peru described in 2008.

children.name: Ameerega berohoka
children.description: Ameerega berohoka is a species of poison dart frog that is endemic to Goiás and Mato Grosso in Brazil.

children.name: Ameerega bilinguis
children.description: Ameerega bilinguis, the Ecuador poison frog, is a species of frogs in the family Dendrobatidae found in Colombia, Ecuador, and possibly Peru.

children.name: Ameerega boehmei
children.description: {{speciesbox | image = Ameerega boehmei 134615760.jpg | status = EN | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | taxon = Ameerega boehmei | authority = , Schmitz, Reichle, Rödder, and Quennet, 2009 | synonyms = | synonyms_ref = }}.

children.name: Ameerega boliviana
children.description: Ameerega boliviana, formerly Epipedobates bolivianus, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae endemic to Bolivia.

children.name: Ameerega braccata
children.description: Ameerega braccata, formerly Epipedobates braccatus, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Ameerega hahneli
children.description: Ameerega hahneli is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Ameerega ignipedis
children.description: Ameerega ignipedis is a species of poison frog found in central Peru.

children.name: Ameerega imasmari
children.description: Ameerega imasmari, commonly known as riddle poison frog, is a species of poison dart frog that lives in Peru and was described in 2019.

children.name: Ameerega ingeri
children.description: Ameerega ingeri, also known commonly as Niceforo's poison frog, Brother Niceforo's poison frog, or Inger's poison frog, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Ameerega labialis
children.description: Ameerega labialis — a species in the genus Ameerega, family Dendrobatidae. Also known as poison dart frogs.

children.name: Ameerega munduruku
children.description: Ameerega munduruku is a species of poison dart frog in the family Dentrobatidae.

children.name: Ameerega panguana
children.description: Ameerega panguana is a species of poison dart frog found in Peru that was first described in 2019.

children.name: Ameerega parvula
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = Ameerega parvula.jpg | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Ameerega | species = parvula | authority = Boulenger, 1882 | synonyms = | synonyms_ref = }}.

children.name: Ameerega pepperi
children.description: Ameerega pepperi is a species of poison frogs found in central Peru.

children.name: Ameerega peruviridis
children.description: Ameerega peruviridis — a species in the genus Ameerega, family Dendrobatidae. Also known as poison dart frogs.

children.name: Ameerega pongoensis
children.description: Ameerega pongoensis, formerly Epipedobates pongoensis, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae that is endemic to the San Martín and Loreto Regions of Peru.

children.name: Ameerega pulchripecta
children.description: Ameerega pulchripecta, formerly Epipedobates pulchripecta, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae endemic to Brazil.

children.name: Ameerega rubriventris
children.description: Ameerega rubriventris is a small species of poison dart frog.

children.name: Ameerega shihuemoy
children.description: Ameerega shihuemoy, the Amarakaeri poison frog, is a species of dart frog endemic to a small region in southeastern Peru in the Manú District where it lives in the transition zone between montane rainforest and the lowland rainforest.

children.name: Ameerega simulans
children.description: Ameerega simulans is a small to medium-sized species of poison dart frog that lives in Peru in Puno described in 1998.

children.name: Ameerega trivittata
children.description: Ameerega trivittata, formerly Epipedobates trivittatus, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae commonly known as the three-striped poison frog.

children.name: Ameerega yoshina
children.description: Ameerega yoshina is a species of poison frogs found in central Peru.

children.name: Ameerega yungicola
children.description: Ameerega yungicola is a species of poison dart frog that lives in Bolivia in the Yungas of La Paz-Bolivia described in 2005.

children.name: Ameerega sp.
children.description: This species adds to the ecological richness of the Amazonian rainforests, known for its vibrant coloring and rapid movement patterns.

children.name: Epipedobates
children.description: Epipedobates — a genus of poison dart frogs in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Epipedobates darwinwallacei
children.description: Epipedobates darwinwallacei — a species in the genus Epipedobates, family Dendrobatidae. Also known as poison dart frogs.

children.name: Epipedobates espinosai
children.description: The Darwin-Wallace poison frog, espinosa poison frog, turquoise-bellied poison frog, or turquoise-bellied poison arrow frog (Epipedobates espinosai) is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae endemic to Ecuador.

children.name: Epipedobates machalilla
children.description: Epipedobates machalilla is a slender species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Epipedobates narinensis
children.description: Epipedobates narinensis is a species of poison dart frog (family Dendrobatidae).

children.name: Epipedobates sp.
children.description: This species brings new diversity to the understory of Central American forests, known for its distinctive patterns and elusive nature.

children.name: Silverstoneia

children.description: Silverstoneia erasmios is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Silverstoneia flotator
children.description: Silverstoneia flotator, the rainforest rocket frog, is a terrestrial, diurnal frog found in humid lowlands of Costa Rica and Panama.

children.name: Silverstoneia gutturalis
children.description: Silverstoneia gutturalis is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Oophaga arborea
children.description: The polkadot poison frog is a species of frogs in the family Dendrobatidae endemic to Panama, where it is known as rana venenosa in Spanish. Its natural habitats are humid lowland and montane forests. It is threatened by habitat loss and is listed by the IUCN as being "critically endangered".

children.description: Category:Taxa named by Eduard Oscar Schmidt.

children.name: Oophaga sylvatica
children.description: Oophaga sylvatica, it is sometimes known Kiki Poison Dart Frog or little devil frog , it is also sometimes known as its Spanish name diablito, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae found in Southwestern Colombia and Northwestern Ecuador.

children.name: Oophaga sp.
children.description: This species brings new diversity to the rainforests of South America, known for its vibrant coloration and elusive behavior.

children.name: Andinobates daleswansoni
children.description: Andinobates daleswansoni is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Andinobates dorisswansonae
children.description: Andinobates dorisswansonae, the dotted poison frog, is a species of amphibian in the family Dendrobatidae, endemic to Colombia in the outskirts of Falan and north of the department of Tolima.

children.name: Andinobates tolimensis
children.description: Andinobates tolimensis, the Tolimense poison frog,  is a species of amphibian in the family Dendrobatidae, endemic to Colombia in the outskirts of Falan and north of the department of Tolima.

children.name: Andinobates victimatus
children.description: Andinobates victimatus — a species in the genus Andinobates, family Dendrobatidae. Also known as poison dart frogs.

children.name: Andinobates viridis
children.description: The green poison frog (Andinobates viridis) is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Andinobates virolinensis
children.description: Andinobates virolinensis (the Santander poison frog) is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Andinobates sp.
children.description: This species brings new diversity to the ecological landscape of South American forests, known for its vibrant coloring and elusive nature.

children.name: Leucostethus
children.description: Leucostethus — a genus of poison dart frogs in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Leucostethus argyrogaster
children.description: Leucostethus argyrogaster or the Imaza rocket frog is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Leucostethus bilsa
children.description: Leucostethus bilsa, the Bilsa white-chested frog, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Leucostethus brachistriatus
children.description: Leucostethus brachistriatus is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Leucostethus fraterdanieli
children.description: Leucostethus fraterdanieli (common name: Santa Rita rocket frog) is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Leucostethus fugax
children.description: {{Speciesbox | status = VU | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | taxon = Leucostethus fugax | authority = (Morales and , 1993) | synonyms = * Colostethus fugax Morales & Schulte, 1993 | synonyms_ref = }}.

children.name: Leucostethus jota
children.description: Leucostethus jota is a species of frog in the family Leucostethus.

children.name: Leucostethus ramirezi
children.description: Leucostethus ramirezi is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae that is endemic to Colombia.

children.name: Leucostethus siapida
children.description: Leucostethus siapida, also called the haunted cuilane, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.

children.name: Leucostethus sp.
children.description: This species adds to the ecological diversity of South American forests, known for its unique coloration and elusive behavior.

children.description: Paruwrobates whymperi, sometimes known as the Tanti rocket frog, is a species of frog in the family Dendrobatidae.
